title: Dashboard redesign — architecture spec and payload mapping (spec only, no code)
gate: Do NOT begin until EXECUTION_HELD_SIDE_SEMANTICS_001 is APPROVED and its review appears in 07_REVIEWS/REVIEW_EXECUTION_HELD_SIDE_SEMANTICS_001.md. Check CLAUDE_TASK_BOARD.md before starting.
objective: Produce a written architecture specification for the full dashboard redesign. This task involves NO code changes. The worker reads the current dashboard.html and dashboard_server.py to understand the existing payload contract, then writes a spec document to 08_SHARED_CONTEXT. The spec must define tabs, focal areas, payload mapping, frontend/backend split, and explicit truth-model preservation rules. All subsequent redesign phases must follow this spec.

truth_model_constraints.current_price_authority: Stream-backed held-side bid only. bid_yes for BUY_YES, bid_no for BUY_NO. Never raw YES price for BUY_NO positions.
truth_model_constraints.fallback_policy: Fallback (last known or REST) only when stream data is stale or missing. Must be labeled as fallback.
truth_model_constraints.shadow_policy: Shadow/advisory data must not appear in the LIVE or POSITIONS tab as a competing truth source. Shadow is diagnostics only.
truth_model_constraints.backed_team_semantics: Positions must display backed_team / faded_team direction, not raw YES/NO contract economics.
truth_model_constraints.no_new_price_writers: The redesign must not introduce any new code path that writes or overrides current_price. The single authority chain is: polymarket stream → state_hub → SSE → frontend.

contents: Tab list with exact names: LIVE, POSITIONS, GAMES, HISTORY, SYSTEM
For each tab: what data it shows, what it demotes, what it must not show
LIVE tab layout: focal area order (live game monitor → live position cards → account strip)
Position card spec: backed_team, faded_team, entry_px, current_held_price, unrealized_pct, live_equity, tp_price, sl_price, size_usd — all named explicitly
Account strip fields: bankroll, capital_committed, open_count, r25.win_rate, r25.expectancy
Game monitor fields: home_team, away_team, inning, score, status, model_confidence (if available), active_position flag
GAMES tab: all live and upcoming games, model shadow recs in clearly labeled secondary context
HISTORY tab: trade log with team names, side direction, PnL, size — NOT on main page
SYSTEM tab: stream health, mark counts, fallback status, process topology, bot loop stats
Payload mapping: for each frontend field, the exact API source (/api/state, /api/games, /api/trades, SSE stream)
Frontend/backend split: what is computed client-side vs server-side
List of any new server fields required and why (must be minimal)
Explicit demotion list: what currently appears above-the-fold that must be removed or moved to secondary tabs
Truth-model preservation checklist: explicit yes/no for each constraint above

preflight_questions: Read dashboard.html in full — what tabs/panels currently exist?
Read dashboard_server.py — what fields does /api/state currently return? What fields does /api/trades return?
What SSE events does the stream currently emit and what fields do they carry?
Which fields in the current UI are sourced from shadow vs paper execution vs stream?
What is the current above-the-fold content and what dominates the mobile view?
acceptance_criteria: Spec defines all 5 tabs with exact names and default
Spec defines the LIVE tab focal area order explicitly
Spec includes a payload mapping table: frontend field → API source
Spec explicitly names current_held_price as the price authority and states its source
Spec includes a truth-model preservation checklist with pass/fail for each constraint
No production code changes — diff of allowed_files is empty
proof_of_work: Include a summary of the 5-tab layout
Include the payload mapping table in the result
Confirm zero production file changes with file diff or explicit statement
out_of_scope: Any changes to truth model or pricing logic
rollback: No code was changed. Delete DASHBOARD_REDESIGN_SPEC_001.md if spec is rejected.
